title: No Fake APIs
rule: AI MUST NOT invent Premiere API methods such as sequence.captions or project.exportCaptions().

title: Preserve Object Identity
rule: AI MUST preserve object identity and must not equate objects by display name.

title: Declare API Layer
rule: Every code answer must explicitly identify the API layer: UXP, ExtendScript, CEP, QE, XML, Python, ffmpeg, or hybrid.

title: Do Not Port AE Assumptions
rule: Premiere scripting must not inherit After Effects scripting assumptions unless explicitly verified.

title: Prefer Official API Before QE
rule: QE-dependent workflows must be treated as fallback or user-approved experimental paths.

title: Preserve Timeline Instance vs Source Distinction
rule: AI must distinguish ProjectItem/source changes from TrackItem/timeline instance changes.

title: Version Gate API Usage
rule: When a method is version-dependent, generated code must include compatibility notes or checks.

title: No Silent Destructive Timeline Edits
rule: Any batch timeline edit must include backup, duplicate sequence, XML export, or explicit rollback guidance.

title: Caption Is Not Plain Text
rule: Caption editing MUST preserve timing, style runs, metadata and run lengths.

title: Validate Caption Run Lengths
rule: When editing rich caption JSON, text length and style/font run lengths must remain consistent.

title: Do Not Flatten Caption Formatting
rule: Caption text replacement must not flatten unknown style runs unless the user explicitly requests plain text output.

title: Do Not Promise Future ExtendScript Development
rule: ExtendScript should be treated as legacy/maintenance-oriented when planning new long-lived Premiere extensions.

title: Distinguish MOGRT Source and Instance
rule: AI must distinguish a MOGRT file/source from a timeline instance with editable parameters.

title: Backup Before Destructive Operations
rule: Production workflows MUST create backup or duplicate sequence before batch mutation.

title: Preserve Unknown Fields
rule: Serializers MUST preserve unknown fields and ordering when modifying structured data.

title: Account For Async Method Calls
rule: UXP code must await asynchronous Premiere methods when required and must not assume ExtendScript synchronous behavior.

title: XML Is Not Full Fidelity
rule: Premiere XML export can omit or transform project state; do not treat XML as a full project backup.
